body:
### Security objective

This control ensures that **encryption keys are rotated within the expected lifecycle window**.

### Lifecycle signal reviewed

The assessment evaluates key rotation metadata and identifies keys whose last rotation is older than the 90-day threshold used by the control.

### Security impact

Rotation limits the useful lifetime of key material and validates that dependent systems can tolerate normal key lifecycle operations. It is especially important for keys protecting sensitive data stores, secrets, or customer-facing workloads.

Automatic rotation should be enabled where supported. For key types that require manual replacement, teams should maintain a tested runbook, keep old key versions available for decryption, and validate dependent services before retiring old material.
name: Ensure encryption keys are rotated
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Rotate the affected key or enable automatic rotation so the key is inside the 90-day rotation window that the control evaluates.

### AWS

1. Open **AWS KMS > Customer managed keys** and select the affected key.
2. If automatic rotation is supported, enable it.
3. If automatic rotation is not supported for the key type, create a replacement key and update dependent services.
4. Validate that services can encrypt and decrypt with the active key.
5. Keep older key material enabled until existing data can still be decrypted.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

CLI command:

```bash
aws kms enable-key-rotation --key-id {{asset.idFromProvider}} --region {{asset.region}}
```

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/rotating-keys.html

The control is satisfied when the key has been rotated within the configured 90-day window.

### GCP

1. Open **Cloud KMS** and select the affected key.
2. Configure a rotation period of 90 days or less.
3. Set the next rotation time.
4. Confirm the new primary key version is enabled after rotation.
5. Validate dependent services can decrypt data encrypted with older enabled versions.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

CLI command:

```bash
gcloud kms keys update {{asset.name}} --keyring <key-ring> --location {{asset.region}} --rotation-period 90d --next-rotation-time <yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ssZ>
```

Reference: https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/rotate-key

The control is satisfied when the key rotation age is 90 days or less.

### Azure

1. Open **Key Vault > Keys** and select the affected key.
2. Configure a key rotation policy with a rotation interval of 90 days or less.
3. Rotate the key or create a new key version.
4. Verify dependent Azure services use the expected key version or versionless key URI.
5. Keep old versions enabled until service validation is complete.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

CLI command:

```bash
az keyvault key rotate --vault-name <vault-name> --name {{asset.name}}
```

Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/keys/how-to-configure-key-rotation

The control is satisfied when the key has been rotated within 90 days.

### Alibaba

1. Open **Key Management Service (KMS)** and select the affected key.
2. Enable automatic rotation if supported by the key type.
3. Otherwise rotate or replace the key manually.
4. Update dependent services to use the active key version where required.
5. Validate encryption and decryption before disabling older key material.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

Reference: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/kms/key-management-service/

The control is satisfied when the key is no longer older than the 90-day rotation threshold.
description: Encryption keys that have not been rotated within the configured 90-day window.
